Title: Ethylene-independent modulation of root development by ACC via downregulation of WOX5 and group I CLE peptide expression
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Quiescent center (QC) of the root apical meristem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QC of root apical meristem; altered after ACC treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signaling molecule that modulates root architecture independently of ethylene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root tissues (site of treatment and action)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACC downregulates WOX5 expression, suppressing root meristem cell proliferation and reducing primary root growth
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACC treatment significantly decreased proWOX5:GFP signal in the QC when ethylene signaling was blocked. 2. ACC altered WOX5 spatial expression, expanding signal proximally and into lower columella tiers in about half of seedlings. 3. Reduced WOX5 expression correlated with smaller RAM, reduced meristematic cell number, reduced EdU incorporation, and supernumerary columella stem cells.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: proWOX5:GFP reporter imaging; fluorescence quantification in QC; root meristem phenotyping; EdU incorporation assay; mPS-PI staining
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ACC treatment (typically 10 µM); ethylene signaling blocked with 1-MCP or analyzed in ein2-5/ET-free backgrounds
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proWOX5 native promoter reporter; 1-MCP used to block ethylene perception
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced primary root elongation and reduced root apical meristem size
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate root meristem activity and primary root growth in response to ACC-related signaling, though reduced primary root growth is a likely tradeoff
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WOX5 was a direct focal readout of ACC action on primary root meristem, but not shown as a nutrient-metabolism gene per se; included because it is a core experimentally studied mediator of ACC signaling.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; ein2-5; acs octuple; cle septuple
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily in cells surrounding lateral root primordia during early LR development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by ACC after prolonged treatment; promoter activity reduced early in LR development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signaling molecule that promotes lateral root development independently of ethylene
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACC suppresses CLE5 expression, contributing to increased lateral root formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq and qRT-PCR showed CLE5 is downregulated by prolonged ACC treatment. 2. proCLE5:GUS activity decreased with ACC, especially early in LR development. 3. acs octuple showed enhanced CLE5 expression, consistent with endogenous ACC repressing CLE5. 4. Group I CLE repression mediates part of ACC-induced LR promotion.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; proCLE5:GUS staining; genetic analysis with cle septuple and acs octuple; exogenous CLE5/6 peptide application
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CLE group I pathway components; CLV1; BAM1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower CLE5 expression is associated with increased LR density, potentially altering root architecture balance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulating CLE5 could be used to enhance lateral root density/root branching, with potential application to nutrient acquisition traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core evidence is mainly for the group I CLE module; individual gene-specific contribution is partially redundant.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily in cells surrounding lateral root primordia during early LR development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by ACC; promoter activity reduced early in LR development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signaling molecule that promotes lateral root development independently of ethylene
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACC suppresses CLE6 expression as part of the group I CLE module that restrains lateral root development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq identified CLE6 as a significantly downregulated group I CLE after 40 h ACC treatment. 2. proCLE6:GUS activity decreased upon ACC treatment. 3. Reduced CLE6 expression is part of the mechanism by which ACC promotes LR development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proCLE6:GUS staining; genetic analysis of cle septuple; exogenous CLE5/6 peptide assays
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CLE group I pathway components; CLV1; BAM1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced CLE6 expression may increase LR density and modify root system allocation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for increasing root branching and potentially nutrient foraging capacity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function interpreted within redundant group I CLE family.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; ein2-5; acs octuple; cle septuple
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily in cells surrounding lateral root primordia during early LR development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by ACC after 36 h; promoter activity reduced during early LR development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signaling molecule modulating root development independently of ethylene
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACC suppresses CLE7 expression, relieving inhibition of lateral root development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CLE7 was significantly downregulated by ACC in RNA-seq and validated by qRT-PCR kinetics. 2. proCLE7:GUS activity decreased in ACC-treated roots. 3. acs octuple showed elevated CLE7 expression, supporting endogenous ACC-mediated repression. 4. CLE7 acts within group I CLEs that negatively regulate LR formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; proCLE7:GUS staining; genetics with acs octuple and cle septuple; peptide rescue assays
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CLE group I pathway components; CLV1; BAM1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced LR branching may alter overall root system distribution
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to increase LR density for improved soil exploration
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence strongest for group-level role rather than exclusive single-gene causality.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: acs2-1; acs4-1; acs5-2; acs6-1; acs7-1; acs9-1; amiRACS8ACS11; ABRC #CS16651
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endogenous ACC biosynthesis genes assessed through loss-of-function mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ACC acts as an ethylene precursor and an ethylene-independent signal regulating root development
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Committed step to ACC formation from S-adenosylmethionine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to ACC, the committed precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed and major control step in ethylene/ACC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ACS function lowers endogenous ACC and decreases lateral root density and length when ethylene perception is blocked; exogenous ACC rescues the phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The acs octuple mutant displayed shorter primary roots and significantly fewer lateral roots than WT under 1-MCP. 2. Exogenous ACC suppressed the reduced LR phenotype of acs octuple, demonstrating ACC dependence. 3. acs octuple showed elevated CLE5 and CLE7 expression, linking endogenous ACC biosynthesis to CLE repression and LR promotion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis (acs octuple; acs octuple;ein2); exogenous ACC rescue; LR phenotyping; qRT-PCR of downstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent aminotransferase-like ACC synthase enzyme family
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1-MCP treatment to block ethylene perception; exogenous ACC
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of ACS reduces LR formation and shortens roots under ethylene-blocked conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating ACC biosynthesis may tune root architecture, but effects are dose-dependent and biphasic for primary root growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats ACS mainly as the source of endogenous ACC; individual ACS isoforms were not dissected here.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed through ET-free mutant lacking all five ACO genes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Canonical hormone derived from ACC; contrasted here with ACC-specific signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ACO abolishes ACC-to-ethylene conversion but ACC still inhibits primary root elongation and promotes LR development, supporting ethylene-independent ACC action
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The ET-free aco quintuple mutant was used to separate ACC effects from ethylene production. 2. ACC inhibited primary root elongation and reduced RAM size in ET-free seedlings. 3. ACC also increased LR density in ET-free seedlings, demonstrating that conversion to ethylene is unnecessary for these responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order loss-of-function mutant (ET-free); root growth phenotyping; meristem measurements; EdU assay; LR density analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ACC; ethylene gas treatments in comparative assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful genetic lever to distinguish ACC signaling from ethylene biosynthesis in engineering studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ACO is a pathway gene central to experimental design, but not itself shown to positively regulate the focal ACC signal.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Early lateral root primordia, pericycle cells, and surrounding cells during LR development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by ACC, especially at Stage I–III LR development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal promoting lateral root development independently of ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation downstream of ACC-mediated repression of group I CLE genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Root developmental signaling linked to ACC-dependent lateral root formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ACC; relief of repression from group I CLE peptides
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: ACC induces LBD18 transcript accumulation; LBD18 promotes downstream genes supporting lateral root emergence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator required for full ACC-induced increase in lateral root density
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LBD18 promotes ACC-induced lateral root development; lbd18 mutants are less responsive to ACC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACC significantly induced LBD18 transcript levels (~3.4-fold), unlike LBD16 or LBD29. 2. proLBD18:GUS showed increased expression in early LR primordia and surrounding cells upon ACC treatment. 3. lbd18 mutants displayed reduced sensitivity to ACC in LR density assays, indicating LBD18 mediates part of ACC's promotive effect on LR formation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; proLBD18:GUS reporter analysis; mutant phenotyping (lbd18) under ACC treatment
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LATERAL ORGAN BOUNDARIES DOMAIN (LBD/ASL) transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing LBD18-dependent branching could alter carbon allocation and overall root system balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance lateral root branching and soil exploration, potentially improving nutrient acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a metabolism gene in the classic sense, LBD18 is a core regulator connecting ACC signaling to root architecture outcomes relevant for nutrient uptake.
